hero.title: 8 Days Nepal.
hero.titleAccent: From Himalayan Peaks to Jungle Rhinos.
hero.intro: Nepal is two countries in one. The Himalayan north — temples, peaks and stupas — and the subtropical south — elephant grass, rhinos and river mist. This is the trip that covers both. Kathmandu's ancient UNESCO sites, Pokhara's Phewa Lake and Annapurna sunrise, then Chitwan's jungle kingdom.
hero.heroImageAlt: Elephant bathing in Chitwan National Park Nepal

excludes: Nepal Visa on Arrival (USD30 for 15 days)

overview.heading: 8 Days Nepal + Chitwan Wildlife Safari: The Full Picture
body: Most Nepal itineraries stay in the Himalayan zone — Kathmandu, Nagarkot, Pokhara. This package adds the dimension that most visitors never discover: the Terai lowlands, where the Himalaya finally meets the Indian plains and subtropical jungle grows thick along the rivers. Chitwan National Park is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and home to the greatest one-horned rhino population in the world, along with Bengal tigers, gharials and over 500 bird species.
The full eight days move from Kathmandu's urban culture through Pokhara's mountain lakes to Chitwan's wild river valleys — three completely different Nepals in one journey.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Chitwan Jungle Jeep Safari
items.body: A morning jeep safari through Chitwan's sal forest and elephant grass — the habitat of the one-horned rhino, spotted deer, langur monkeys and if luck holds, the elusive Bengal tiger. One of the best wildlife experiences in Asia.
items.imageAlt: Chitwan National Park Nepal jeep safari elephant

items.title: Rapti River Canoe Ride
items.body: A dugout canoe drift on the Rapti River at the jungle's edge. Gharial crocodiles on the sandbanks, kingfishers on the branches and the sound of the jungle completely surrounding you. Peaceful and utterly unlike anything in Kathmandu.
items.imageAlt: Rapti River Chitwan Nepal canoe wildlife

items.title: Pokhara, Phewa Lake & Annapurna Sunrise
items.body: Before the jungle, two days in Nepal's most beautiful lake town. The Annapurna range reflected in Phewa Lake at dawn, a Sarangkot sunrise and a boat ride to the World Peace Stupa.
items.imageAlt: Phewa Lake Pokhara Nepal Machapuchare reflection

days.title: Arrive Kathmandu
days.description: Arrive at Tribhuvan International Airport and transfer to your Kathmandu hotel. Evening at leisure in Thamel.

days.title: Kathmandu UNESCO Temples
days.description: Full day visiting Kathmandu's UNESCO sites — Pashupatinath, Boudhanath, Swayambhunath and Durbar Square.

days.title: Bhaktapur & Fly to Pokhara
days.description: Morning in medieval Bhaktapur, then fly to Pokhara. Afternoon at leisure by Phewa Lake.

days.title: Pokhara: Sarangkot Sunrise & Lakeside
days.description: Pre-dawn drive to Sarangkot for the Annapurna sunrise, then a full day exploring Pokhara.

days.title: Drive to Chitwan National Park
days.description: Drive from Pokhara to Chitwan (approximately 4 hours). Afternoon jungle orientation walk and Tharu village visit.

days.title: Chitwan Full Jungle Day
days.description: Early morning jeep safari, Rapti River canoe ride, elephant bathing observation and a nature walk in the national park buffer zone.

days.title: Chitwan to Kathmandu
days.description: Final morning in Chitwan, then transfer back to Kathmandu by road or flight.

days.title: Depart Kathmandu
days.description: Free morning in Kathmandu, then transfer to the airport for your onward flight.

whyBook.heading: Nepal's Peaks and Jungles, Seamlessly Connected
whyBook.body: Linking the Himalayan north with the Terai jungle south requires logistics that most travellers do not think about until it is too late — timing the drive to Chitwan, the right jeep guide for wildlife, the lodge that places you where the animals are. Dhesu has been running this circuit since 1988.

sections.heading: Chitwan National Park Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
Chitwan National Park in Nepal's southern Terai lowlands is one of Asia's great wildlife experiences — and almost unknown among Malaysian travellers. The park protects the world's second-largest population of one-horned rhinos (after Kaziranga in India), along with Bengal tigers, gharials, wild elephants and over 500 bird species.

The combination of a Nepal Himalayan circuit with a Chitwan add-on gives you the single most complete experience of what this extraordinary country has to offer — from the high mountain world of Kathmandu and the serenity of Pokhara to the subtropical jungle of the Terai.

sections.heading: What to Expect at Chitwan
sections.body:
The park covers 952 square kilometres of sal forest, tall elephant grass and river floodplain. Jeep safaris enter the core zone in the early morning, when animals are most active. Rhino sightings are almost guaranteed in the right season. Tiger sightings are rarer but possible. The Rapti River canoe trip gives a completely different perspective — slow, quiet and intimate.

The Tharu people are the indigenous inhabitants of the Chitwan valley, and their cultural village visits and stick-dance performances are a genuine and respectful addition to the wildlife experience.

items.question: Will we definitely see rhinos in Chitwan?
items.answer: One-horned rhino sightings are very common in Chitwan — the park has over 600 individuals and they are seen on the vast majority of jeep safaris, particularly in the morning. Tiger sightings are rarer but possible. Leopard, wild elephant, sloth bear, gharial and hundreds of bird species are also regularly encountered.

items.question: How do we get from Pokhara to Chitwan?
items.answer: The drive from Pokhara to Chitwan takes approximately 4 to 5 hours by private car through scenic lowland terrain. There is also a domestic flight option (35 minutes). Your private vehicle and driver handle all inter-city transfers throughout the package.

items.question: Is Chitwan suitable for children?
items.answer: Yes, for children aged 6 and above. Jeep safaris are safe and comfortable. The canoe ride is gentle. The Tharu village and stick dance are very family-friendly. Young children will likely find the elephant bathing observation a particular highlight.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Nepal?
items.answer: Malaysian passport holders obtain a Visa on Arrival at Kathmandu's Tribhuvan International Airport. A 15-day tourist visa costs USD 30. Bring passport photos and the fee in cash. Dhesu provides full guidance with your booking.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Chitwan?
items.answer: October to March is the best window for Chitwan. The jungle is drier, the grass has been cut in January (which improves visibility), and wildlife concentrates near the river. April and May are hot but good for tiger sightings. The park partially closes June to September during monsoon.
